rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions or using valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for such assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either visible features or properly declared helpers within the same annex.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to prevent resolution errors.
Validate that guarantee expressions have consistent types on both sides of operators to avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes, not in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for such contracts.
